heading: Grants for certain trauma centers
label: 300d–41 Grants for certain trauma centers

children.text: to assist in defraying substantial uncompensated care costs;

to further the core missions of such trauma centers, including by addressing costs associated with patient stabilization and transfer, trauma education and outreach, coordination with local and regional trauma systems, essential personnel and other fixed costs, and expenses associated with employee and non-employee physician services; and
to provide emergency relief to ensure the continued and future availability of trauma services.

children.heading: Minimum qualifications of trauma centers
children.label: b Minimum qualifications of trauma centers

children.heading: Participation in trauma care system operating under certain professional guidelines
children.label: 1 Participation in trauma care system operating under certain professional guidelines
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the Secretary may not award a grant to a trauma center under subsection (a) unless the trauma center is a participant in a trauma system that substantially complies with .section 300d–13 of this title

children.heading: Exemption
children.label: 2 Exemption
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to trauma centers that are located in States with no existing trauma care system.

children.heading: Qualification for substantial uncompensated care costs
children.label: 3 Qualification for substantial uncompensated care costs
i
children.text: At least 40 percent of the visits in the emergency department of the hospital in which the trauma center is located were charity or self-pay patients.

children.label: ii
children.text: At least 50 percent of the visits in such emergency department were Medicaid (under title XIX of the Social Security Act ( et seq.)) and charity and self-pay patients combined.42 U.S.C. 1396

children.label: i
children.text: At least 35 percent of the visits in the emergency department were charity or self-pay patients.

children.label: ii
children.text: At least 50 percent of the visits in the emergency department were Medicaid and charity and self-pay patients combined.

children.label: i
children.text: At least 20 percent of the visits in the emergency department were charity or self-pay patients.

children.label: ii
children.text: At least 30 percent of the visits in the emergency department were Medicaid and charity and self-pay patients combined.

children.heading: Trauma centers in 1115 waiver States
children.label: 4 Trauma centers in 1115 waiver States
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (3), the Secretary may award a substantial uncompensated care grant to a trauma center under subsection (a)(1) if the trauma center qualifies for funds under a Low Income Pool or Safety Net Care Pool established through a waiver approved under section 1115 of the Social Security Act ().42 U.S.C. 1315

children.heading: Designation
children.label: 5 Designation
children.text: The Secretary may not award a grant to a trauma center unless such trauma center is verified by the American College of Surgeons or designated by an equivalent State or local agency.
submits to the Secretary a plan satisfactory to the Secretary that demonstrates a continued commitment to serving trauma patients regardless of their ability to pay; and
has policies in place to assist patients who cannot pay for part or all of the care they receive, including a sliding fee scale, and to ensure fair billing and collection practices.
